title: $VVV FUD Debunked: Why Big Tech Can't Just 'Checkbox' Privacy
summary: A viral tweet by @galactiator dismantles the claim that Big Tech can easily add privacy as a checkbox feature, calling it historically illiterate and pointing out that if it were that simple, companies would have already done it.
body: A tweet by @galactiator (G.W. Jackston) challenges the notion that Big Tech can quickly implement privacy as a simple checkbox feature. The post argues that this claim is not only historically inaccurate but also ignores the fundamental challenges of privacy implementation. The tweet points out that if privacy were as easy as flipping a switch, companies would have already done it. The author references historical examples and practical obstacles, suggesting that the reality is far more complex than the FUD (fear, uncertainty, and doubt) suggests. This debate matters because it affects how we trust and use technology. If privacy were truly a simple checkbox, we might not need robust regulations or decentralized solutions. However, the reality is that privacy requires systemic changes and ongoing efforts, not just a quick fix. For those interested in the details, the tweet thread provides a deeper dive into the historical and technical nuances. It's worth following the conversation to understand the complexities involved in achieving true privacy in technology.
twitterThread: Big Tech can't just 'checkbox' privacy. If it were that easy, they'd have done it already. The claim is historically illiterate.
The tweet by @galactiator dismantles the FUD piece by piece, starting with the load-bearing claim that privacy is a simple checkbox.

For the full breakdown, check out the thread. It's a must-read for understanding the complexities of privacy in tech Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-07-vvv-fud-debunked-why-big-tech-cant-just-checkbox-privacy
standaloneTweet: Privacy isn't a checkbox Big Tech can just add. If it were, they'd have done it by now. The FUD doesn't hold up.
